text.title: Egress Cost Hidden Budget Guide Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Q3 bill 2x Q2; cost-explorer shows DataTransfer doubled; nobody knew.
antipattern.reason: No egress tracking; no per-feature attribution.
antipattern.note: Fix: Build sources table. Attribute each to a feature. Set caps + alerts. Add CloudFront / VPC endpoints where applicable.

antipattern.description: All egress attributed to 'platform'; optimisation targets nothing in particular.
antipattern.note: Fix: Trace each source to a feature: media-downloads, read-replicas, cross-region-backup, etc.

antipattern.description: NAT-GW processing $3k/month; S3 traffic 80% of it.
antipattern.reason: S3 access via NAT-GW (default route); no VPC endpoint.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add com.amazonaws.us-east-1.s3 gateway endpoint. Update route tables. NAT-GW processing drops ~80%.

antipattern.description: Marketing campaign triggers 5x traffic; egress bill spikes; budget breached mid-month.
antipattern.reason: Budget set to average; worst-case never modelled.
antipattern.note: Fix: Model 4x peak; reserve budget for it. Add freeze trigger at 100% to halt traffic-amplifying ops.

antipattern.description: Budget set in 2024 for 100k users; product now has 500k; budget breached every month.
antipattern.note: Fix: Schedule quarterly review tied to release cadence; update caps per traffic delta.
